published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 + standard finite LP vertex-attainment claim
RichardsonRobins2010 fixed-defier IV geometry; SachsJonzonSjolanderGabriel2023 symbolic LP bounds; DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 automated discrete sharp bounds
BalkePearl1997 and RichardsonRobins2010 certify sharpness through latent response distributions
ImbensAngrist1994 LATE; Manski1990 bounded-outcome support bounds

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The RichardsonRobins2010 comparison is incomplete: verified secondary descriptions say they bound effects for all compliance types and the whole population as a function of the known defier proportion; since pi_N = q10 - pi_F, a never-taker upper cap is a lower bound on pi_F, so §5/§8 must explain why optimizing those fixed-defier bounds over the cap-compatible interval does not subsume the proposed envelope.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist misses closer generic symbolic/automated bound precedents: Sachs-Jonzon-Sjolander-Gabriel 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1080/10618600.2022.2071905) and Duarte-Finkelstein-Knox-Mummolo-Shpitser 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.2023.2216909), both directly target sharp bounds in discrete/linear-program causal models.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the current kernel reads as an analytic specialization of known fixed-defier/symbolic-LP IV geometry, not yet a cited open-regime resolution, strict wider-class theorem, or estimator-frontier equivalence/non-equivalence result.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a concrete comparison theorem or table showing whether the two-cap endpoint equals optimization of Richardson-Robins fixed-defier bounds over t in [max{0,q10-nu_bar,q10-q00}, min{q01,q10,delta_bar}], and isolate the exact leftover novelty if it does not.

Add Sachs-Jonzon-Sjolander-Gabriel and Duarte-Finkelstein-Knox-Mummolo-Shpitser to §5, then state what their symbolic/automated algorithms would output versus what the proposed hand facet catalogue proves that they do not.

To reach flagship tier, sharpen Conjecture 1 into a necessary-and-sufficient observable strict-shrinkage frontier tied to Bai et al.'s no-power result, with an explicit open-set regime where the never-taker cap has identifying power not reducible to fixed-defier sensitivity.

recommended_next_step: Revise §5 and the Conjecture 1 novelty block around Richardson-Robins plus symbolic/automated bounds, then strengthen the strict-shrinkage frontier enough to clear the flagship floor.
